title: A Maxillary Sinus Lift Technique by using New Maintained Active Bioline Implants-The Case Report
author: Ashwati1, Budda Jeevan Krishna2, Neelam Bhandari3, Renuka4*, Mrunmayee Soman5 and Ashwini Sukanya Gunasekar6
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Tooth extraction of maxillary posteriors often results in pneumatization of the sinus. As a result of this, there can be height and width reduction in the posterior region. Severe atrophy in the maxillary posterior bone caused by maxillary sinus pneumatization may cause difficulty in implant placement. It is essential that the sinus must be elevated in such cases. This will facilitate implant placement effectively. This case report describes the placement of implants in maxillary posteriors with the use of an autogenous bone graft for a sinus lift procedure which helps avoid the complexities usually faced with a direct sinus lift.
